text.title: White-Label Partnership Design Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every White-Label Partnership Design application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: White-label rate ≥1.5-2× direct rate. Reasoning: client-facing comms, revisions overhead, brand-suppression all add cost.
rule.rationale: Industry rule-of-thumb: below 1.5× direct rate, white-label is a net loss after support costs.

rule.statement: No single primary agency contributes >30% of revenue. Beyond = effectively captive.
rule.rationale: Captivity caps protect against single-agency departure = business failure; 30% is the empirically safe ceiling.

rule.statement: Contract: primary agency commits to no circumvent (going direct to your contractors). You commit to no direct-client poaching.
rule.rationale: Trust collapses under circumvention; explicit clause is the only safeguard.

rule.statement: Contract clarifies: no brand attribution (white-label) OR limited (e.g., 'powered by' in fine print). Surprise attribution kills the partnership.
rule.rationale: Brand-attribution surprise is the #1 white-label dispute; explicit policy prevents.

rule.statement: Quarterly review with primary agency: workload, quality, revenue share, future projects. Maintains relationship + surfaces friction.
rule.rationale: Without review, friction accumulates silently until rupture; quarterly cadence is the safety valve.

rule.statement: If all Applies-If preconditions hold and no Skip-If disqualifier is present, execute the full procedure from `04-procedure.xml` and commit the artefact described in `02-output-contract.xml`.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's affirmative leaf; pairs the preconditions with the methodology's commit path.

rule.statement: If any Skip-If condition is present (one-shot, <3 instances/year, regulator override, missing owner), do NOT apply this methodology. Route to the sibling/upstream methodology named in `AGENTS.md` Related section.
rule.rationale: Anchor for the decision tree's skip leaf; prevents over-application of a heavyweight artefact when the loop conditions do not hold.
